nodes.label: Tests for runner/filesystem security hardening.  Covers: - session workspace dir
Return a sandbox policy with ``active=False`` (type=none).      :returns: An ina
Build a fake agent spec with ``sandbox.type="none"``.      :param cwd: Working d
Build a fake agent spec whose ``cwd`` is a placeholder.      The registry will s
Workspace dirs created by the registry use mode 0700.      :param tmp_path: Pyte
A symlink pointing outside cwd is rejected.      :param tmp_path: Pytest-provide
A symlink that resolves within cwd is allowed.      :param tmp_path: Pytest-prov
A symlink chain using ``..`` to escape is rejected.      :param tmp_path: Pytest
The read op in ``_handle_helper_request`` rejects symlink escapes.      :param t
The write op rejects symlink escapes.      :param tmp_path: Pytest-provided temp
The edit op rejects symlink escapes.      :param tmp_path: Pytest-provided tempo
Normal relative paths within the workspace are allowed.      :param tmp_path: Py
Sessions get isolated subdirectories when per_session_workspace=True.      :para
Without per_session_workspace, sessions share the runner workspace.      :param
Per-session subdirectories are created with mode 0700.      :param tmp_path: Pyt
``per_session_workspace=False`` lands sessions at the workspace root.      :para
Default keeps per-session workspace isolation for shared-host runners.      :par
Requests without an auth header are rejected with 401.
Requests with the wrong token are rejected with 401.
Requests with the correct token pass through to the route.
GET /health succeeds without any auth token.
When auth_token is None, no middleware is installed.
